content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

Using the array AAA, we define the variables S1S_1S1​ and S2S_2S2​:


Select an index iii (1≤i<N)(1\le i \lt N)(1≤i<N);
Define S1=(A1⊕A2⊕…⊕Ai)S_1 = (A_1 \oplus A_2 \oplus \ldots \oplus A_i)S1​=(A1​⊕A2​⊕…⊕Ai​) ∣|∣ (A(i+1)⊕…⊕AN)(A_{(i+1)} \oplus \ldots \oplus A_N)(A(i+1)​⊕…⊕AN​), where ⊕\oplus⊕ and ∣|∣ denote the bitwise XOR and bitwise OR operations respectively;
Define S2=(A1⊕A2⊕…⊕Ai)S_2 = (A_1 \oplus A_2 \oplus \ldots \oplus A_i)S2​=(A1​⊕A2​⊕…⊕Ai​) &\&& (¬(A(i+1)⊕…⊕AN))(\neg(A_{(i+1)} \oplus \ldots \oplus A_N))(¬(A(i+1)​⊕…⊕AN​)), where ⊕,\oplus,⊕, &,\&,&, and ¬\neg¬ denote the bitwise XOR, bitwise AND, and bitwise NOT operations respectively.
Your task is to **maximise** the value (S1−S2)(S_1 - S_2)(S1​−S2​) by selecting an optimal value of iii.

**Note:** Each element is regarded as an unsigned 323232-bit integer. Thus, if X=0X=0X=0, ¬X=4294967295\neg X=4294967295¬X=4294967295, which can also be written as (232−1)(2^{32}-1)(232−1). Similarly, if X=1X=1X=1, ¬X=4294967294\neg X=4294967294¬X=4294967294, which can also be written as (232−2)(2^{32}-2)(232−2).

You should answer QQQ queries. Each query is of the form:


XXX YYY: Given index XXX (1≤X≤N)(1\le X \le N)(1≤X≤N) and an integer YYY, set AX:=YA_X := YAX​:=Y and find the **maximum** value of (S1−S2)(S_1 - S_2)(S1​−S2​) possible.
Each query is **independent**. In other words, for each query, we modify on the original array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ, the size of AAA and the number of queries.
The next line contain NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The next QQQ lines describe the queries. For the ithi^{th}ith (1≤i≤Q)(1\le i \le Q)(1≤i≤Q) line, there are two space-separated integers XXX and YYY, describing that for this query, we need to set AX=YA_X = YAX​=Y.


Output Format
For each query, output on a new line, the **maximum** value of (S1−S2)(S_1-S_2)(S1​−S2​), you can get after modification.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 111: We set A4=2A_4 = 2A4​=2. Thus, the array is A=[1,2,3,2]A=[1,2,3,2]A=[1,2,3,2]. The optimal way is to select i=1i = 1i=1. We get:

S1=1S_1 = 1S1​=1 ∣|∣ (2⊕3⊕2)=3(2\oplus 3\oplus 2) = 3(2⊕3⊕2)=3
S2=1S_2 = 1S2​=1 &\&& (¬(2⊕3⊕2))=1(\neg(2\oplus 3\oplus 2)) = 1(¬(2⊕3⊕2))=1 &\&& (¬3)=1(\neg3) = 1(¬3)=1 &\&& 4294967292=04294967292 = 04294967292=0

Thus, (S1−S2)=3(S_1 - S_2) = 3(S1​−S2​)=3. It can be shown that this is the maximum value of S1−S2S_1-S_2S1​−S2​ possible for any (1≤i<N)(1\le i \lt N)(1≤i<N).
Query 222: We set A3=0A_3 = 0A3​=0. Thus, the array is A=[1,2,0,4]A=[1,2,0,4]A=[1,2,0,4]. The optimal way is to select i=1i = 1i=1. We get:

S1=1S_1 = 1S1​=1 ∣|∣ (2⊕0⊕4)=7(2\oplus 0\oplus 4) = 7(2⊕0⊕4)=7
S2=1S_2 = 1S2​=1 &\&& (¬(2⊕0⊕4))=1(\neg(2\oplus 0\oplus 4)) = 1(¬(2⊕0⊕4))=1 &\&& (¬6)=1(\neg6) = 1(¬6)=1 &\&& 4294967289=14294967289 = 14294967289=1

Thus, (S1−S2)=7−1=6(S_1 - S_2) = 7 - 1 = 6(S1​−S2​)=7−1=6. It can be shown that this is the maximum value of S1−S2S_1-S_2S1​−S2​ possible for any (1≤i<N)(1\le i \lt N)(1≤i<N).
Query 333: We set A4=7A_4 = 7A4​=7. Thus, the array is A=[1,2,3,7]A=[1,2,3,7]A=[1,2,3,7]. The optimal way is to select i=3i = 3i=3. We get:

S1=(1⊕2⊕3)S_1 = (1\oplus 2\oplus 3)S1​=(1⊕2⊕3) ∣|∣ 7=77 = 77=7
S2=(1⊕2⊕3)S_2 = (1\oplus 2\oplus 3)S2​=(1⊕2⊕3) &\&& (¬7)=0(\neg7) = 0(¬7)=0 &\&& (¬7)=0(\neg7) = 0(¬7)=0

Thus, (S1−S2)=7−0=7(S_1 - S_2) = 7 - 0 = 7(S1​−S2​)=7−0=7. It can be shown that this is the maximum value of S1−S2S_1-S_2S1​−S2​ possible for any (1≤i<N)(1\le i \lt N)(1≤i<N).
